leadText: The Battle Resort (Japanese: バトルリゾート Battle Resort) is a location in Hoenn. It only appears in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. It is the counterpart to Pokémon Ruby and Sapphire's Battle Tower and Pokémon Emerald's Battle Frontier, and is their predecessor. It is only accessible after the player obtains the S.S. Ticket, which is obtained from Norman in Littleroot Town after completing the Delta Episode. It is accessed via the S.S. Tidal from the port in Slateport City or Lilycove City.

sections.title: Role
sections.text: Role [ edit | edit source ] When the player arrives for the first time, they are greeted by Wally , who had also received an S.S. Ticket and an invitation to the resort. Following him, the player encounters Archie , Matt , and Shelly OR / Maxie , Tabitha , and Courtney AS . The trio thank the player for stopping their nemesis, and rewards them with Sharpedonite OR / Cameruptite AS . Upon entering the main area, the player and Wally are given an introduction to the Battle Resort by a Collector ; if the player does not already have one, he will give them the Vs. Recorder . On the southern side of the island, Looker has washed ashore with a bad case of amnesia. A local woman takes him in to her cabin. If the player visits him here, he gives the player an Audinite which he found in his coat pocket. At the southeast corner of the island, there is a Triathlete who is looking for a new Bike . This Triathlete is one of three NPCs the player needs to talk to in order to be able to obtain both Bikes from Rydel .

sections.title: Places of interest
sections.text: Contents 1 Places of interest 1.1 Pokémon Day Care Annex 1.2 Stats judge 1.3 Sound system 1.4 Battle Maison Replica 1.5 Grunt couple 1.6 Whismur show 1.7 Move Tutors Places of interest [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Day Care Annex [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Pokémon Day Care An annex of the Pokémon Day Care Center on Route 117 is located to the east of the Pokémon Center . It possesses all the services of the Pokémon Day Care on the mainland. It is run by the Day Care Lad and his older sister and holds Pokémon separately from the one on Route 117, allowing for two pairs of Pokémon to be bred at once. Additionally, there is a continuous cycling path for hatching and breeding by standing in front of the Day Care Lad in front of the Day Care and riding sideways (both left and right work) until the player hits a corner, then holding up and cycling for as long as needed. Stats judge [ edit | edit source ] Inside the Pokémon Center of the resort, there is a stats judge who will read the individual values (IVs) of a selected Pokémon and determine the range of values which the IVs fall under, as well as naming all of its stats that have an IV of 31 or 0 (and make clear which is which). Sound system [ edit | edit source ] South of the Pokémon Day Care Annex is a Parasol Lady who will offer the change the background music, accepting which will change the background music of the Battle Resort outside to one of several tracks at random. The music will remain the area's background music until she changes it again, even if the player leaves the area. Using any theme other than the Battle Resort theme, cycling and surfing will not change the music. If the player is cycling and changes the theme to Battle Resort, the music that plays at the time will be the cycling theme (although dismounting will result in the Battle Resort theme playing, as normal). The available tracks are: Battle Resort Hurry Along Victory Road Rival 's Theme Secret Super Training ! Battle Maison Replica [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Battle Maison There is a replica of the Kalos Battle Maison in the center of the Battle Resort. Progressing far enough through each battle mode will result in a battle against one of the Battle Chatelaines . Grunt couple [ edit | edit source ] In the second cabin to the right of the Battle Maison there is a male Team Magma Grunt and a female Team Aqua Grunt who have fallen in love and abandoned their teams to be together. While they agree to stay together no matter how their teams decide to punish them, two other grunts arrive to assure them that will be fine, and offer their blessings. After leaving and entering again, the couple tell the player that they have decided to return to their respective teams, hoping to help mediate the tension between the teams. As a sign of their vow, the Team Magma Grunt and Team Aqua Grunt give the player their Camerupt and Sharpedo , respectively, that they had raised in honor of the leaders they admired. Whismur show [ edit | edit source ] Northwest of the Battle Maison, there is a woman with four Whismur who will perform a cup-and-ball mini-game. The objective of the game is to guess which Whismur is holding a Big Mushroom . If the player identifies the correct Whismur, they win the Big Mushroom. If the player loses, they may try again until the correct guess is made. The player can only receive one Big Mushroom per day this way. On rare occasions, the game may be played with seven more Whismur and, oddly, a Marill. Eight of the Whismur leave after the game, along with the one holding the Big Mushroom, who later switches places with Marill. This minigame is similar to the Patrat show in Pokémon Black and White and the elemental monkey show in Pokémon Black 2 and White 2 . Move Tutors [ edit | edit source ] Move Tutors are available at the Battle Resort, immediately left of the Pokémon Center. Four Tutors have set up stands next to each other, and will teach Pokémon moves in exchange for Battle Points . Leftmost Move Type Cat.
